name: Class Act
description_short: Class Act Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union based in Louisville, KY, offering personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, and checking accounts to its members.
description_long:
Class Act Federal Credit Union operates as a federally-chartered credit union serving members in the Louisville, Kentucky area. The organization maintains multiple branch locations, including branches at Jeffersontown High School, Southern High School, and Doss High School, providing in-person service to its membership. As a credit union, Class Act is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration).

The credit union offers a range of consumer financial products including personal loans, auto loans (both new and used vehicles with terms up to 84 months), boat and RV loans, mortgages for home purchase, and deposit accounts including an Honors Checking product. Their auto loan rates advertised on the website range from 4.49% APR for new vehicles (up to 48 months) to 5.49% APR (up to 84 months), and boat/RV loans starting at 6.24% APR. They also offer a Lifestyle Access Line of Credit at rates as low as 9.75% APR. The credit union emphasizes online account opening and maintains digital banking services alongside traditional branch banking.

Class Act differentiates itself through its school-based branch locations, making services accessible to youth and families in educational settings. The credit union explicitly positions itself as a lifelong financial partner, with messaging about serving members "from their first youth account" through major life milestones like home purchases. Their website includes proactive member education, including warnings about online loan scams and guidance on tax refund deposits. The organization appears committed to accessibility, featuring screen-reader mode and accessibility widgets on their website.

As a credit union, Class Act offers member-owned advantages typical of the credit union model, though specific details about membership requirements, fee structures, and minimum balances are not available on their website. The multiple branch closures listed suggest seasonal and weather-related service interruptions that members should plan around. Prospective members should verify membership eligibility requirements and compare rates with competing institutions, as the advertised rates shown are promotional rates with asterisks indicating terms and conditions apply.
services: Auto loans for new vehicles (up to 48 months at 4.49% APR, up to 84 months at 5.49% APR)
Boat and RV loans (up to 84 months at 6.24% APR)
Lifestyle Access Line of Credit (9.75% APR)
Multiple branch locations for in-person service
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.49% APR for new vehicles with up to 48-month terms
Multiple physical branch locations including school-based branches for accessibility (Jeffersontown, Southern, Doss High Schools)
Offers comprehensive loan products including personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, boat/RV loans, and lines of credit
Member education resources and fraud awareness warnings prominently displayed on website
Online account opening and digital banking services available alongside branch banking
Not-for-profit credit union structure provides member-owned advantages
Youth account offerings positioning the institution for lifelong member relationships
cons: Multiple branch closures throughout the year for holidays and weather limit in-person service availability
Limited information on the website regarding membership eligibility requirements and fee structures
Advertised rates contain asterisks indicating terms, conditions, and likely qualification requirements not fully disclosed on the homepage
Website content does not specify minimum account balances, membership fees, or other cost details
No information provided about specific APR ranges for personal loans, making rate comparison difficult
best_for: Louisville area residents seeking to finance vehicle purchases with competitive APR rates
Students and families with access to school-based branch locations (Jeffersontown, Southern, Doss High Schools)
Members seeking comprehensive financial services from a not-for-profit, member-owned institution
Borrowers interested in boat, RV, or mortgage products alongside traditional personal and auto loans
diagnosis: Class Act Federal Credit Union is best for Louisville-area residents and students who want competitive loan rates and member-owned financial services with convenient branch access, particularly those near the school-based locations. The main caveat is that membership eligibility requirements are not disclosed on their website, and advertised rates contain undisclosed terms and conditions that may not apply to all borrowers—interested parties should contact the credit union directly to confirm rates, fees, and membership requirements before opening an account.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Class Act — Credit Union in Louisville, KY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Class Act — NCUA-insured credit union in Louisville, KY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
